steps.title: Locate the angle on the unit circle
steps.content: The angle $\frac{3\pi}{4}$ radians equals $\frac{3}{4} \times 180° = 135°$. This places the angle in the second quadrant, between $90°$ and $180°$.

steps.title: Identify the cosine sign and reference angle
steps.content: In the second quadrant, cosine values are negative. The reference angle is $\pi - \frac{3\pi}{4} = \frac{\pi}{4}$, and we know $\cos\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}$.

steps.title: Apply the negative sign
steps.content: Since the angle is in the second quadrant where cosine is negative, apply the negative sign to the reference angle's cosine: $$\cos\left(\frac{3\pi}{4}\right) = -\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
